insight: Ivory Coast's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been underwhelming, with the team failing to gain momentum in both of their breaks, resulting in a negative average change of -0.150 minutes per break. This suggests that the breaks have actually hindered their progress, rather than providing an opportunity to regroup and refocus. Specifically, when Ivory Coast was pressing before a break, they not only failed to maintain their momentum after the break, but also saw a decline in their performance, with an average change of -0.150 minutes per break, indicating that the breaks disrupted their rhythm and intensity. Overall, the data indicates that hydration breaks have had a negative impact on Ivory Coast's performance, and the team may need to adjust their strategy during these breaks to regain their momentum and stay competitive.
